claim: The test suite shall partition every collected test into exactly one of two responsibility tiers -- 'framework' (exercising internal/ mechanics) or 'domain' (asserting concrete self-domain content) -- so the framework tier is a separately selectable, self-contained subset.
why: Steward doctrine (verdict #8, verbatim): being-working is the framework's OWN responsibility, its tests run 'до всего отдельно' -- first and separately. That is only possible if the framework tests are a named, selectable subset. Not yet implemented: the suite runs via `go test ./...` with no tier-partition mechanism -- there is no marker registry, no build-tag partition, and no collection-modification hook. (Historically the Python suite tagged each test framework-vs-domain via a centralized, auditable DOMAIN_COUPLED registry in spec/tests/conftest.py, making the framework tier selectable via `pytest -m framework` and the domain tier via `pytest -m domain`; before that partition the suite hard-coded self-domain content (C3): under a foreign active-domain pin 18 tests reddened because they assumed self-domain atoms/structure.) PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists.
